summary: DHH announces that Highrise, a CRM product spun off from Basecamp as a separate company in 2014, is being reabsorbed back into Basecamp. The spin-off experiment under CEO Nathan Kontny and a team of seven is ending, though the technical operations were always handled by Basecamp's team. DHH emphasizes continuity for customers, noting the original builders are still at Basecamp and the product will be supported indefinitely. The post frames the move as a natural homecoming rather than a failure, and hints at new future directions for the product.
key_points: Highrise operated as a separate company for about four years but its infrastructure was always run by Basecamp's team
The spin-off is characterized as an experiment that had highlights but ultimately led to a different strategic decision
Basecamp commits to supporting Highrise 'until the end of the internet' for its 10,000+ customers
The transition is framed as nearly invisible to customers since the original team never really left
money_quotes: Spinning Highrise off into a separate company with a separate team and CEO was always a bit of an experiment.
For customers of Highrise, we at Basecamp are committed to supporting the product until the end of the internet!
Highrise is home to over 10,000 customers who've trusted their data and their business to us.
In short, this transition back to the Basecamp team is going to be nearly invisible to Highrise customers.
Highrise is returning to the team that originally built the software, and it's being operated on the technical side by the same team that always did so.
key_insight: Sometimes the best outcome of a business experiment is recognizing when to bring things back home, prioritizing customer continuity over organizational novelty.
